388	class MinTokensLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
389	
390	    def __init__(self, pin_memory: bool, device: torch.device):
391	        # index -> (min_toks, output_token_ids, stop_token_ids)
392	        super().__init__()
393	        self.min_toks: dict[int, tuple[int, Sequence[int], set[int]]] = {}
394	        self.device = device
395	        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
396	
397	        # (req_idx_tensor,eos_tok_id_tensor)
398	        self.logits_slice: tuple[torch.Tensor,
399	                                 torch.Tensor] = (self._device_tensor(
400	                                     [], torch.int32),
401	                                                  self._device_tensor(
402	                                                      [], torch.int32))
403	
404	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
405	        """By censoring stop tokens, min-tokens can change the outcome
406	        of the argmax operation in greedy sampling."""
407	        return False
408	
409	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
410	        needs_update = False
411	
412	        if batch_update:
413	            # Process added requests.
414	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.added)
415	            for index, params, output_tok_ids in batch_update.added:
416	                if (isinstance(params, SamplingParams)
417	                        and (min_tokens := params.min_tokens)
418	                        and len(output_tok_ids) < min_tokens):
419	                    # Replace request metadata at batch index
420	                    self.min_toks[index] = (min_tokens, output_tok_ids,
421	                                            params.all_stop_token_ids)
422	                else:
423	                    # Drop request metadata at batch index
424	                    self.min_toks.pop(index, None)
425	
426	            if self.min_toks:
427	                # Process removed requests.
428	                for index in batch_update.removed:
429	                    if self.min_toks.pop(index, None):
430	                        needs_update = True
431	
432	                # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and
433	                # swapped (a<->b)
434	                for a_index, b_index, direct in batch_update.moved:
435	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL:
436	                        if (a_entry := self.min_toks.pop(a_index,
437	                                                         None)) is None:
438	                            if self.min_toks.pop(b_index, None) is not None:
439	                                needs_update = True
440	                        else:
441	                            self.min_toks[b_index] = a_entry
442	                            needs_update = True
443	                    else:
444	                        a_entry = self.min_toks.pop(a_index, None)
445	                        if (b_entry := self.min_toks.pop(b_index,
446	                                                         None)) is not None:
447	                            self.min_toks[a_index] = b_entry
448	                            needs_update = True
449	                        if a_entry is not None:
450	                            self.min_toks[b_index] = a_entry
451	                            needs_update = True
452	
453	        if self.min_toks:
454	            # Check for any requests that have attained their min tokens.
455	            to_remove = tuple(index for index, (min_toks, out_tok_ids,
456	                                                _) in self.min_toks.items()
457	                              if len(out_tok_ids) >= min_toks)
458	            if to_remove:
459	                needs_update = True
460	                for index in to_remove:
461	                    del self.min_toks[index]
462	
463	        # Update tensors if needed.
464	        if needs_update:
465	            reqs: list[int] = []
466	            tok_ids: list[int] = []
467	            for req, (_, _, stop_tok_ids) in self.min_toks.items():
468	                reqs.extend([req] * len(stop_tok_ids))
469	                tok_ids.extend(stop_tok_ids)
470	
471	            self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor(reqs, torch.int32),
472	                                 self._device_tensor(tok_ids, torch.int32))
473	
474	    def _device_tensor(self, data: list, dtype: torch.dtype) -> torch.Tensor:
475	        return (torch.tensor(data,
476	                             device="cpu",
477	                             dtype=dtype,
478	                             pin_memory=self.pin_memory).to(device=self.device,
479	                                                            non_blocking=True))
480	
481	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
482	        if self.min_toks:
483	            # Inhibit EOS token for requests which have not reached min length
484	            logits[self.logits_slice] = -float("inf")
485	        return logits
486	
487	
488	def init_builtin_logitsprocs(pin_memory_available: bool, max_num_reqs: int,
489	                             device: torch.device) -> LogitsProcessorManager:
490	    """Construct 'builtin' vLLM logitsprocs which the engine
491	    loads by default.
492	
493	    Args:
494	      pin_memory_available: pinned memory is available for use
495	                            for use by logitsproc
496	      max_num_reqs: ceiling on request count in persistent batch
497	      device: inference device
498	
499	    Returns:
500	      Data structure encapsulating loaded logitsprocs
501	    """
502	    min_tokens_logitproc = MinTokensLogitsProcessor(
503	        pin_memory=pin_memory_available, device=device)
504	    logit_bias_logitproc = LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(
505	        pin_memory=pin_memory_available, device=device)
506	    min_p_logitproc = MinPLogitsProcessor(
507	        pin_memory=pin_memory_available,
508	        device=device,
509	        # +1 for temporary swap space
510	        max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1)
511	    return LogitsProcessorManager(
512	        non_argmax_invariant=[
513	            min_tokens_logitproc,
514	            logit_bias_logitproc,
515	        ],
516	        argmax_invariant=[min_p_logitproc],
517	    )

22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()

27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """

40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:

42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")

59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "

63	                self.forward = self.forward_native

66	        else:

82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)

89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""

120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209	
210	
211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits
234	
235	
236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)

169	class Sampler(nn.Module):
170	    """Samples the next tokens from the model's outputs.
171	
172	    This layer does the following:
173	    1. Discard the hidden states that are not used for sampling (i.e., all
174	        tokens except the final one in each prompt).
175	    2. Compute the logits for the next tokens.
176	    3. Apply presence, frequency and repetition penalties.
177	    4. Apply temperature scaling.
178	    5. Apply top-p and top-k truncation.
179	    6. Sample the next tokens.
180	    Here, each sequence group within the batch can have different sampling
181	    parameters (e.g., sampling method, temperature, top-p, top-k, etc.).
182	
183	    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
184	    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
185	    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a
186	    prompt request with the prompt_logprobs sampling parameter, there are rows
187	    in logits for each token in the input prompt.
188	    """
189	
190	    def __init__(self):
191	        super().__init__()
192	
193	        # Whether or not the SamplerOutput should have on-device tensors
194	        # containing the sampled token ids and probabilities. This is used by
195	        # speculative decoding and when prompt embeddings are specified.
196	        self.include_gpu_probs_tensor = False
197	        self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace = False
198	
199	    def _init_sampling_tensors(
200	        self,
201	        logits: torch.Tensor,
202	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
203	    ):
204	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
205	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
206	        decodes of the same sequences.
207	        """
208	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
209	
210	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
211	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
212	        # have pinned memory.
213	        self._sampling_tensors = None
214	
215	        # Initialize new sampling tensors
216	        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
217	         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
218	             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
219	
220	        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
221	        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
222	        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
223	        self._do_min_p = do_min_p
224	
225	    def forward(
226	        self,
227	        logits: torch.Tensor,
228	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
229	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
230	        """
231	        Single-step scheduling:
232	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
233	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
234	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
235	
236	        Multi-step scheduling:
237	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
238	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
239	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
240	            tensor
241	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
242	            in the
243	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
244	            structure
245	
246	        Args:
247	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
248	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
249	        """
250	        assert logits is not None
251	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
252	
253	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
254	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
255	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
256	        elif self._do_penalties:
257	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
258	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
259	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
260	            # between decode runs.
261	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
262	
263	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
264	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
265	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
266	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
267	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
268	
269	        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
270	
271	        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
272	        if do_penalties:
273	            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
274	                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
275	                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
276	                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
277	                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
278	
279	        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
280	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
281	        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
282	        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
283	
284	        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
285	            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
286	                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)
287	
288	        if do_min_p:
289	            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
290	
291	        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
292	        # Compute the probabilities.
293	        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
294	        # Compute the log probabilities.
295	        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
296	
297	        # Sample the next tokens.
298	        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
299	            probs,
300	            logprobs,
301	            sampling_metadata,
302	            sampling_tensors,
303	            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
304	            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
305	        )
306	
307	        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
308	            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
309	            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
310	            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
311	            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
312	            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
313	        else:
314	            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve
315	            # GPU-side tensors.
316	            on_device_tensors = None
317	
318	        # Get the logprobs query results.
319	        prompt_logprobs = None
320	        sample_logprobs = None
321	        if not sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output:
322	            # Pythonize logprobs now (GPU -> CPU); do not defer.
323	            assert not isinstance(maybe_deferred_sample_results,
324	                                  SampleResultArgsType)
325	            prompt_logprobs, sample_logprobs = get_logprobs(
326	                logprobs, sampling_metadata, maybe_deferred_sample_results)
327	
328	        return _build_sampler_output(
329	            maybe_deferred_sample_results,
330	            sampling_metadata,
331	            prompt_logprobs,
332	            sample_logprobs,
333	            on_device_tensors=on_device_tensors,
334	            skip_sampler_cpu_output=sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output)
335	
336	    @property
337	    def _should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace(self) -> bool:
338	        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
339	        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
340	        the greedily-sampled token.
341	
342	        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
343	        sampled token to 1.
344	
345	        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
346	        method be encoded into the probability distribution.
347	        """
348	        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace
349	
350	
351	def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
352	    logits: torch.Tensor,
353	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
354	) -> torch.Tensor:
355	    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
356	        have not been generated yet
357	    """
358	    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
359	    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
360	    logits_applied = 0
361	    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
362	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
363	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
364	
365	        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
366	        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
367	            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
368	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
369	            continue
370	
371	        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
372	        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
373	        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
374	        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
375	            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
376	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
377	                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
378	                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
379	                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)
380	
381	            if seqs_to_penalize:
382	                # convert to the index into logits
383	                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
384	                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
385	                logits_to_penalize.extend(
386	                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))
387	
388	    if logits_to_penalize:
389	        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
390	        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
391	        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")
392	
393	    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
394	    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
395	    return logits
396	
397	
398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits
425	
426	
427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits
442

444	def _greedy_sample(
445	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
446	    samples: torch.Tensor,
447	) -> SampleResultType:
448	    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.
449	
450	    Args:
451	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
452	        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
453	            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
454	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
455	    Returns:
456	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
457	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
458	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
459	    """
460	    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
461	    sample_idx = 0
462	    results: SampleResultType = []
463	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
464	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
465	            results.append(([], []))
466	            continue
467	
468	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
469	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
470	        assert num_parent_seqs == 1, (
471	            "Greedy sampling should have only one seq.")
472	        parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
473	        next_token_ids = [samples_lst[sample_idx]]
474	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
475	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
476	    return results
477

27	def _create_fake_logits(batch_size: int, vocab_size: int) -> torch.Tensor:

32	def _create_penalty_tensor(batch_size: int, penalty_value: float,
33	                           device: torch.device) -> torch.Tensor:

40	def _create_prompt_tokens_tensor(
41	    prompt_token_ids: list[list[int]],
42	    vocab_size: int,
43	    device: torch.device,
44	) -> torch.Tensor:
45	    return make_tensor_with_pad(
46	        prompt_token_ids,
47	        pad=vocab_size,
48	        device=device,
49	        dtype=torch.int64,
50	        pin_memory=False,
51	    )

53	
54	def _create_allowed_token_ids(
55	    batch_size: int,
56	    vocab_size: int,
57	    num_allowed_token_ids: int,
58	    device: torch.device,
59	) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
60	    mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
61	    for i in range(batch_size):
62	        if i % 2 == 1:
63	            continue
64	        if mask is None:
65	            mask = torch.zeros((batch_size, vocab_size),
66	                               dtype=torch.bool,
67	                               device=device)
68	        start = min(i, vocab_size - 1)
69	        end = min(i + num_allowed_token_ids, vocab_size - 1)
70	        mask[i, start:end] = True
71	    return mask
72

74	def _create_bad_words_token_ids(
75	        batch_size: int, vocab_size: int,
76	        bad_words_lengths: list[tuple[int]]) -> dict[int, list[list[int]]]:
77	    bad_words_token_ids = {}
78	    for batch_idx in range(batch_size):
79	        token_ids_single_batch = []
80	        for bad_words_length in bad_words_lengths:
81	            token_ids = np.random.choice(vocab_size,
82	                                         size=bad_words_length,
83	                                         replace=True).tolist()
84	            token_ids_single_batch.append(token_ids)
85	        bad_words_token_ids[batch_idx] = token_ids_single_batch
86	    if batch_size >= 2:
87	        # Test no bad_words for some batch
88	        no_bad_words_batch_idx = np.random.choice(batch_size)
89	        bad_words_token_ids.pop(no_bad_words_batch_idx, None)
90	    return bad_words_token_ids

93	def _update_output_token_ids_for_bad_words(
94	        metadata: SamplingMetadata, vocab_size: int) -> dict[int, list[int]]:
95	    bad_words_last_tokens = {}
96	    for batch_idx, bad_words_token_ids in metadata.bad_words_token_ids.items():
97	        output_token_ids = metadata.output_token_ids[batch_idx]
98	        bad_words_last_token: list[int] = []
99	        for i, bad_word_token_ids in enumerate(bad_words_token_ids):
100	            if len(bad_word_token_ids) == 1:
101	                # Single token id always affects logits
102	                bad_words_last_token.append(bad_word_token_ids[0])
103	            else:
104	                prefix_length = len(bad_word_token_ids) - 1
105	                has_bad_words = np.random.choice([True, False])
106	                if has_bad_words:
107	                    output_token_ids[-prefix_length:] = bad_word_token_ids[:-1]
108	                    bad_words_last_token.append(bad_word_token_ids[-1])
109	                    break  # Maximum one update to output_token_ids
110	                else:  # Make sure no accidental match to bad words
111	                    output_token_ids[-1] = (bad_word_token_ids[-2] +
112	                                            1) % vocab_size
113	        bad_words_last_tokens[batch_idx] = bad_words_last_token
114	    return bad_words_last_tokens

25	    def forward(
26	        self,
27	        logits: torch.Tensor,
28	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
29	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
30	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
31	        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
32	        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
33	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
34	        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
35	        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
36	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
37	        if num_logprobs is not None:
38	            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
39	
40	        # Use float32 for the logits.
41	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
42	        # Apply allowed token ids.
43	        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
44	        # Apply bad words exclusion.
45	        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
46	
47	        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
48	        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
49	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
50	
51	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
52	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
53	        # Sample the next token.
54	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
55	        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
56	        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
57	        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
58	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
59	        sampled = sampled.long()
60	
61	        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
62	        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
63	        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
64	            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
65	
66	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
67	        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)
68	
69	        # These are GPU tensors.
70	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
71	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
72	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
73	            # token per request.
74	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
75	            logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors,
76	        )
77	        return sampler_output

80	        self,
81	        logits: torch.Tensor,
82	        temp: torch.Tensor,
83	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
84	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
85	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))

87	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
88	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)

90	    def sample(
91	        self,
92	        logits: torch.Tensor,
93	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
94	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
95	        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.
96	
97	        The various logits processing functions called in this method
98	        may update the logits tensor in-place.
99	        """
100	
101	        assert not (sampling_metadata.all_greedy
102	                    and sampling_metadata.all_random)
103	        if sampling_metadata.all_random:
104	            greedy_sampled = None
105	        else:
106	            greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
107	            if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
108	                return greedy_sampled
109	
110	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
111	
112	        # Apply temperature.
113	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
114	
115	        # Apply logits processors that only apply to random sampling
116	        # (argmax invariant)
117	        for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
118	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
119	
120	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
121	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
122	            logits,
123	            sampling_metadata.generators,
124	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
125	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
126	        )
127	
128	        if greedy_sampled is None:
129	            return random_sampled
130	
131	        sampled = torch.where(
132	            sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
133	            greedy_sampled,
134	            random_sampled,
135	            out=greedy_sampled,  # Reuse tensor
136	        )
137	        return sampled

## Comprehensive Report: vLLM Sampling Filter Implementation

### File Locations

**Primary V1 Sampler Files:**
1. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331152632-9tzvkm/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` - Main V1 sampler module
2. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331152632-9tzvkm/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` - Top-k/top-p filter implementation
3. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331152632-9tzvkm/vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` - Logits processor framework
4. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331152632-9tzvkm/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` - Sampling metadata structure

**Legacy V0 Sampler Files:**
1. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331152632-9tzvkm/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` - V0 sampler (legacy)

---

### Sampling Pipeline Execution Order (V1)

The complete filtering and sampling pipeline in `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` executes in this order:

**In `forward()` method (lines 25-77):**
1. **Line 41:** Convert logits to float32
2. **Line 43:** Apply allowed token IDs (mask out disallowed tokens)
3. **Line 45:** Apply bad words exclusion
4. **Lines 48-49:** Apply non-argmax-invariant logits processors (min_tokens, logit_bias)
5. **Line 52:** Apply penalties (frequency, presence, repetition)
6. **Line 54:** Call `sample()` method (contains temperature and top-k/top-p)

**In `sample()` method (lines 90-137):**
1. **Line 106:** Compute greedy sample (argmax)
2. **Line 113:** Apply temperature scaling: `logits / temperature`
3. **Lines 117-118:** Apply argmax-invariant logits processors (min_p)
4. **Lines 121-126:** Apply top-k and top-p filtering via `TopKTopPSampler`
5. **Line 257 in topk_topp_sampler:** Random sampling from filtered logits

---

### Top-K Filter Implementation

**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

**Function: `apply_top_k_only(logits, k)` (lines 211-233)**

**Purpose:** Filter to keep only the k highest probability tokens

**Tensor Operations:**
```python
# Line 225: Get the k-th largest value using topk
top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())

# Line 232: Set all logits below k-th value to -inf
logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
```

**Key Details:**
- Uses `torch.topk()` to find the k largest values
- Does NOT sort the entire vocabulary (more efficient than top-p)
- Uses `masked_fill_()` to set non-top-k tokens to `-inf` (in-place operation)
- Handles edge case where k equals vocab size

---

### Top-P Filter Implementation

**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

**Function: `apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)` (lines 168-208)**

**Purpose:** Filter to keep tokens that comprise the top p fraction of probability mass

**Tensor Operations for Top-P (lines 197-204):**
```python
# Line 199: Convert sorted logits to probabilities
probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)

# Line 200: Compute cumulative sum of probabilities
probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)

# Line 201: Identify tokens where cumulative prob exceeds threshold
top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)

# Line 203: Ensure at least one token is kept
top_p_mask[:, -1] = False

# Line 204: Set filtered tokens to -inf
logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
```

**Order of Operations:**
1. Lines 187: Sort logits in ascending order to enable cumulative sum
2. Lines 189-195: Apply top-k filter first (if k is provided)
3. Lines 197-204: Apply top-p filter (if p is provided)
4. Line 207: Re-scatter sorted logits back to original positions

**Key Details:**
- Sorts entire vocabulary (O(n log n) operation)
- Uses cumulative sum to identify cutoff point
- **Ensures at least 1 token remains** (line 203: `top_p_mask[:, -1] = False`)
- In-place modifications to `logits_sort` tensor

---

### Random Sampling After Filtering

**Function: `random_sample(probs, generators)` (lines 236-257)**

**Tensor Operations:**
```python
# Line 245: Create exponential random noise
q = torch.empty_like(probs)

# Line 251: Fill with exponential distribution (for batch without custom generators)
q.exponential_()

# Line 257: Gumbel-max trick: divide and argmax
return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
```

**Key Details:**
- Uses **Gumbel-max trick** for efficient sampling from filtered distribution
- Avoids CPU-GPU synchronization (unlike `torch.multinomial`)
- Supports per-request custom generators (lines 250-256)
- Returns flattened 1D tensor of selected token IDs

---

### Metadata Structure

**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` (lines 12-44)

**SamplingMetadata Dataclass:**
```python
@dataclass
class SamplingMetadata:
    temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]          # shape: (batch,)
    all_greedy: bool
    all_random: bool
    top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]                # shape: (batch,)
    top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]                # shape: (batch,)
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator]       # per-request seeds

    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor            # shape: (batch,)
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor             # shape: (batch,)

    output_token_ids: list[list[int]]            # per-request token history
    allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor]  # shape: (batch, vocab)
    bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]]
    logitsprocs: LogitsProcessorManager
```

---

### Logits Processor Framework

**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`

**Abstract Base Class: `LogitsProcessor` (lines 175-206)**

```python
class LogitsProcessor(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Apply filter to logits, may modify in-place"""
        
    @abstractmethod
    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        """True if doesn't affect greedy sampling (argmax)"""
        
    @abstractmethod
    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]) -> None:
        """Called before each forward pass when batch changes"""
```

**Two Categories:**
1. **Non-argmax-invariant (lines 512-514):** Applied before temperature scaling
   - `MinTokensLogitsProcessor`
   - `LogitBiasLogitsProcessor`

2. **Argmax-invariant (line 516):** Applied after temperature scaling
   - `MinPLogitsProcessor`

---

### Where to Insert Top-A Filter

**Recommended Location: In `TopKTopPSampler.forward_native()` (line 81)**

Current code (lines 69-83):

## Comprehensive Report: vLLM Sampling Filter Implementation

### File Locations

**Primary V1 Sampler Files:**
1. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331152632-9tzvkm/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` - Main V1 sampler module
2. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331152632-9tzvkm/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` - Top-k/top-p filter implementation
3. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331152632-9tzvkm/vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` - Logits processor framework
4. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331152632-9tzvkm/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` - Sampling metadata structure

**Legacy V0 Sampler Files:**
1. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331152632-9tzvkm/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` - V0 sampler (legacy)

---

### Sampling Pipeline Execution Order (V1)

The complete filtering and sampling pipeline in `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` executes in this order:

**In `forward()` method (lines 25-77):**
1. **Line 41:** Convert logits to float32
2. **Line 43:** Apply allowed token IDs (mask out disallowed tokens)
3. **Line 45:** Apply bad words exclusion
4. **Lines 48-49:** Apply non-argmax-invariant logits processors (min_tokens, logit_bias)
5. **Line 52:** Apply penalties (frequency, presence, repetition)
6. **Line 54:** Call `sample()` method (contains temperature and top-k/top-p)

**In `sample()` method (lines 90-137):**
1. **Line 106:** Compute greedy sample (argmax)
2. **Line 113:** Apply temperature scaling: `logits / temperature`
3. **Lines 117-118:** Apply argmax-invariant logits processors (min_p)
4. **Lines 121-126:** Apply top-k and top-p filtering via `TopKTopPSampler`
5. **Line 257 in topk_topp_sampler:** Random sampling from filtered logits

---

### Top-K Filter Implementation

**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

**Function: `apply_top_k_only(logits, k)` (lines 211-233)**

**Purpose:** Filter to keep only the k highest probability tokens

**Tensor Operations:**
```python
# Line 225: Get the k-th largest value using topk
top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())

# Line 232: Set all logits below k-th value to -inf
logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
```

**Key Details:**
- Uses `torch.topk()` to find the k largest values
- Does NOT sort the entire vocabulary (more efficient than top-p)
- Uses `masked_fill_()` to set non-top-k tokens to `-inf` (in-place operation)
- Handles edge case where k equals vocab size

---

### Top-P Filter Implementation

**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

**Function: `apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)` (lines 168-208)**

**Purpose:** Filter to keep tokens that comprise the top p fraction of probability mass

**Tensor Operations for Top-P (lines 197-204):**
```python
# Line 199: Convert sorted logits to probabilities
probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)

# Line 200: Compute cumulative sum of probabilities
probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)

# Line 201: Identify tokens where cumulative prob exceeds threshold
top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)

# Line 203: Ensure at least one token is kept
top_p_mask[:, -1] = False

# Line 204: Set filtered tokens to -inf
logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
```

**Order of Operations:**
1. Lines 187: Sort logits in ascending order to enable cumulative sum
2. Lines 189-195: Apply top-k filter first (if k is provided)
3. Lines 197-204: Apply top-p filter (if p is provided)
4. Line 207: Re-scatter sorted logits back to original positions

**Key Details:**
- Sorts entire vocabulary (O(n log n) operation)
- Uses cumulative sum to identify cutoff point
- **Ensures at least 1 token remains** (line 203: `top_p_mask[:, -1] = False`)
- In-place modifications to `logits_sort` tensor

---

### Random Sampling After Filtering

**Function: `random_sample(probs, generators)` (lines 236-257)**

**Tensor Operations:**
```python
# Line 245: Create exponential random noise
q = torch.empty_like(probs)

# Line 251: Fill with exponential distribution (for batch without custom generators)
q.exponential_()

# Line 257: Gumbel-max trick: divide and argmax
return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
```

**Key Details:**
- Uses **Gumbel-max trick** for efficient sampling from filtered distribution
- Avoids CPU-GPU synchronization (unlike `torch.multinomial`)
- Supports per-request custom generators (lines 250-256)
- Returns flattened 1D tensor of selected token IDs

---

### Metadata Structure

**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` (lines 12-44)

**SamplingMetadata Dataclass:**
```python
@dataclass
class SamplingMetadata:
    temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]          # shape: (batch,)
    all_greedy: bool
    all_random: bool
    top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]                # shape: (batch,)
    top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]                # shape: (batch,)
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator]       # per-request seeds
    max_num_logprobs: Optional[int]
    no_penalties: bool
    prompt_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor            # shape: (batch,)
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor             # shape: (batch,)
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor           # shape: (batch,)
    output_token_ids: list[list[int]]            # per-request token history
    allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor]  # shape: (batch, vocab)
    bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]]
    logitsprocs: LogitsProcessorManager
```

---

### Logits Processor Framework

**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`

**Abstract Base Class: `LogitsProcessor` (lines 175-206)**

```python
class LogitsProcessor(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Apply filter to logits, may modify in-place"""
        
    @abstractmethod
    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        """True if doesn't affect greedy sampling (argmax)"""
        
    @abstractmethod
    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]) -> None:
        """Called before each forward pass when batch changes"""
```

**Two Categories:**
1. **Non-argmax-invariant (lines 512-514):** Applied before temperature scaling

   - `LogitBiasLogitsProcessor`

2. **Argmax-invariant (line 516):** Applied after temperature scaling

top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
    to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
    to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.

if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:

class SamplingParams(
        msgspec.Struct,
        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
        # required for @cached_property.
        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
    """Sampling parameters for text generation.

    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.

    Args:
        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
            tokens.
        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
            repeat tokens.
        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
            the model to repeat tokens.
        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
            the stop tokens are special tokens.
        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
            can complete the sequence.
        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
            output text. Defaults to False.
        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
            a first argument.
        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
            Defaults to None.
        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
            sampling implementations.
    """

    n: int = 1
    best_of: Optional[int] = None
    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    top_k: int = 0
    min_p: float = 0.0
    seed: Optional[int] = None
    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
    min_tokens: int = 0
    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.
    # It is not exposed in the OpenAI API server, as the OpenAI API does
    # not support returning only a list of token IDs.
    detokenize: bool = True
    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
    spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type. We use Any here because
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type is not supported by msgspec.
    logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
    output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE

    # The below fields are not supposed to be used as an input.
    # They are set in post_init.
    output_text_buffer_length: int = 0
    _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)

    # Fields used to construct logits processors
    guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
    logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None

    # Fields used for bad words
    bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
    _bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None

    @staticmethod
    def from_optional(
        n: Optional[int] = 1,
        best_of: Optional[int] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
        repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        temperature: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        top_k: int = 0,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
        bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None,
        include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False,
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16,
        min_tokens: int = 0,
        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
        prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
        detokenize: bool = True,
        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
        logits_processors: Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] = None,
        truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int,
                                                   msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None,

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional

import torch

from vllm.v1.sample.logits_processor import LogitsProcessorManager


@dataclass
class SamplingMetadata:

    temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    all_greedy: bool
    all_random: bool

    top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]

    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator]

    # None means no logprobs, 0 means sampled token logprobs only
    max_num_logprobs: Optional[int]

    no_penalties: bool
    prompt_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor

    output_token_ids: list[list[int]]

    # `allowed_token_ids_mask` is a 2D bool tensor of shape (max batch size,
    # vocab size).
    allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor]

    # req_index -> bad_words_token_ids
    bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]]

    # Loaded logits processors
    logitsprocs: LogitsProcessorManager

1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
3	
4	from typing import Optional
5	
6	import torch
7	import torch.nn as nn
8	
9	from vllm import envs
10	from vllm.logger import init_logger
11	from vllm.platforms import current_platform
12	
13	logger = init_logger(__name__)
14	
15	try:
16	    import flashinfer.sampling
17	    is_flashinfer_available = True
18	except ImportError:
19	    is_flashinfer_available = False
20	
21	
22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
41	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
65	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
66	        else:
67	            self.forward = self.forward_native
68	
69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
75	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        """
77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
84	
85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
108	
109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
118	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
119	
120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209	
210	
211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits
234	
235	
236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
297
